content:
💡 Frosty | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: To significantly improve Android device battery life by optimizing and restricting background Google services and other power-hungry processes.
✨ Key Features: • GMS Freezing across 8 categories • App Doze and Deep Doze for aggressive background restrictions • Screen Off Optimization (disabling connections, RAM cleaner) • Kill Google Tracking (analytics, telemetry, ad tracking) • Kernel Tweaks (scheduler, VM, network)
📖 Summary: Frosty is an advanced Android module designed to significantly enhance battery life by freezing Google Mobile Services (GMS) and implementing system-wide doze optimizations. It offers granular control over GMS categories, app dozing, background restrictions, and screen-off optimizations, all configurable via a WebUI.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 opentrashmail | PHP
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a self-hosted, flexible, and temporary email receiving service for various applications, including testing, automation (like 2FA), and privacy-focused sign-ups.
✨ Key Features: • Python-powered mail server • RSS feed for every email address • JSON API for integration • Webhooks with customizable payloads • Handles attachments
📖 Summary: Open Trashmail is a self-hosted, open-source solution for managing temporary email addresses. It provides a mail server that can receive emails and make them accessible via a web interface, JSON API, RSS feed, and custom webhooks, without requiring pre-creation of email addresses.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ ultra | C#
🎯 Primary Use Case: Performance profiling and analysis of .NET applications on Windows and macOS.
✨ Key Features: • Sampling profiler (ETW on Windows, EventPipe on macOS ARM64) • UI based on https://profiler.firefox.com/ • Timeline, Flamegraph, Call Tree, Marker Chart visualizations • Precise kernel, native, and managed function call stacks • .NET GC Memory track
📖 Summary: Ultra is an advanced profiler for .NET applications on Windows and macOS, providing detailed performance insights through sampling and ETW/EventPipe data. It visualizes performance data using a UI similar to Firefox Profiler, offering features like timeline, flamegraph, and call tree views.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 Automated-AI-Web-Researcher-Ollama | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To automate the process of online research by using an LLM to identify research topics, gather information from the web, and synthesize findings.
✨ Key Features: • Automated research planning with prioritized focus areas • Systematic web searching and content analysis • All research content and source URLs saved into a detailed text document • Research summary generation
📖 Summary: This Python repository transforms a locally run LLM via Ollama into an automated web researcher. It takes a single query, breaks it down into researchable focus areas, performs web searches, scrapes content, and compiles findings into a document. The LLM can then summarize the research and answer questions about it.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ pdf-rag-app | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling users to have conversational interactions with the content of PDF documents.
✨ Key Features: • RAG application for chatting with PDF files • Vectorizes uploaded PDFs • Uses pre-trained models for text generation • ReactJS and TypeScript frontend • FastAPI backend
📖 Summary: PageSage is a web application that enables users to interact with PDF documents by leveraging Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG). It allows users to upload PDFs, which are then vectorized and processed by a language model to answer user queries within the context of the document.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
